name: Create order from basket

targetRestContract.notes: CheckoutModel.OnPost is [Authorize] so this is always an authenticated operation. The legacy handler hardcodes the shipping address as new Address('123 Main St.', 'Kent', 'OH', 'United States', '44240') — the REST target MUST accept a real address from the request body. The service returns void; the REST target should return HTTP 201 with the new order ID.

notes: The shipping address is HARDCODED in CheckoutModel.OnPost in the legacy app — the REST target must accept it from the client in the request body.
CatalogItemOrdered stores a snapshot of the item name and picture URI at order time — changes to the catalog later do NOT affect historical orders.

Guard.Against.EmptyBasketOnCheckout throws EmptyBasketOnCheckoutException if basket.Items is empty — CheckoutModel catches this and redirects to /Basket/Index. REST target should return 422 Unprocessable Entity.
Guard.Against.Null(basket) throws if the basket is not found — REST target should return 404.
The service returns Task (void) — no order ID is returned to the caller. The REST target should return 201 Created with the order ID from the inserted row.
Caller always calls SetQuantities before CreateOrderAsync to sync quantities, then calls DeleteBasketAsync after — this endpoint only creates the order.
